channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: EU mutual assistance clause is unambiguous: Macron
item.description: The EU's mutual assistance clause is unambiguous, French President Emmanuel Macron said on Saturday, after the bloc's leaders asked officials to prepare a blueprint for how it would work amid doubts over Washington's commitment to NATO. President Donald Trump's criticism of NATO for failing to bac Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: BBC will cut up to 2,000 jobs to reduce costs by about 10pc
item.description: The BBC said Wednesday that it plans to cut up to 2,000 jobs to save 10% of its annual budget - 500 million pounds ($677 million) - over the next two years. The layoffs announced during a call with staff are the biggest in more than a decade at the U.K. national broadcaster. "I know this creates Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy arrives in Jordan for next leg of Gulf tour
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy arrived in Jordan ​on Sunday as part of a ‌Gulf tour aimed at shoring up defence ties with countries in the region amid ​rising tensions over the Iran ​war. Zelenskiy is seeking support from Gulf ⁠states for Ukraine's war against Russia ​as Western mili Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: France detains two more suspects over foiled Paris Bank of America attack
item.description: French authorities have arrested two more suspects over a ​foiled attack on Bank of America’s Paris offices, the country’s anti-terrorism prosecutor’s office said ‌on Sunday. “Two further individuals were taken into police custody last night as part of the investigatio Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: London Jewish community ambulances set ablaze in antisemitic attack, PM says
item.description: Four ambulances belonging ​to a Jewish community organisation in north London were set ablaze overnight in what British Prime ‌Minister Keir Starmer called "a deeply shocking antisemitic arson attack". The London Fire Brigade, which sent six fire engines and 40 firefighters to the scene, said Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Russia says Ukraine controls 15–17 per cent of Donetsk region
item.description: The Russian-installed head of ​Ukraine's Donetsk region, ‌Denis Pushilin, told Russian president Vladimir Putin ​at a ​meeting shown on state ⁠TV on Tuesday ​that Ukraine controls around ​15-17 per cent of the region. Russia claims the whole of ​the Donetsk region as part ​of Russia and says Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Greece sees deal to buy Italian-made frigates by April, minister says
item.description: Greece hopes to wrap up talks and sign a deal ​for the purchase of Bergamini class ‌frigates from Italy by April, Defence Minister Nikos Dendias said in an interview ​released on Sunday. High-ranking officials from ​Greece and Italy last year signed memorandums for ⁠naval cooperation, opening Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to meet EU's von der Leyen to push for restart of Druzhba oil flows
item.description: Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico said on Sunday he ‌would meet with EU chief Ursula von der Leyen on Tuesday, as Slovakia and Hungary ramp up pressure on Ukraine to restart Russian oil flows, warning Slovakia could block a major EU ​loan to Kyiv. Russian oil flows through the Druzhba pipelin Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Explosion hits US embassy in Oslo, causing minor damage, police say
item.description: The US embassy in Oslo was hit by an explosion early on Sunday, causing minor damage but no injuries, Norwegian police said. A loud blast was heard near the embassy compound in western Oslo at around 1am (0000 GMT), and smoke was seen rising from the area, eyewitnesses told local media. "We've de Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Plane carrying newly printed currency crashes, killing 15
item.description: Lockheed C-130 Hercules cargo plane carrying newly printed Bolivian banknotes crashed Friday near the capital, leaving at least 15 people dead, injuring others and damaging numerous vehicles, officials said. Defense Minister Marcelo Salinas said the aircraft skidded off the runway while landing at Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Buses, trams and trains grind to a halt across Germany at start of 2-day strike
item.description: Buses, trams and trains across Germany ground to a halt early Friday as local transport workers heeded a call by the Verdi public sector union to stage a strike on Feb 27 and 28. The union is aiming to gain leverage in negotiations that cover working conditions, specifically working hours and shif Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Cuba situation escalating, humanitarian needs must be solved: Kremlin
item.description: The Kremlin said on Thursday that the situation in Cuba was heating up and called for restraint after a deadly incident with a Florida-registered speedboat off the coast of the Caribbean island. Cuban forces killed four exiles and wounded six others who sailed into Cuban waters aboard a speedboat Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Iran now defines Strait of Hormuz as far larger zone, IRGC officer says
item.description: Iran has expanded its definition of the Strait of Hormuz into a “vast operational area” far wider than before the Iran war, according to a senior officer in the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy. The strait is no longer viewed as a narrow stretch around a handful of islands Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 200 sanctioned Russian ships entered UK waters
item.description: Almost 200 so-called Russian "shadow fleet" vessels have entered UK waters since the prime minister threatened to intercept them nearly seven weeks ago, BBC Verify analysis suggests. In March, Sir Keir Starmer announced that British armed forces "are now able to board sanctioned vessels that are p Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Modi urges limits on fuel use, travel and imports to save forex
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday urged a spate of measures including fuel conservation, work-from-home practices and limits on travel and imports, as a surge in global energy prices puts pressure on the country's foreign exchange reserves. People should prioritise a return to work-fro Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Pakistan protests to Afghanistan over suicide attack that killed 15
item.description: Pakistan on Monday summoned a senior Afghan diplomat and lodged a formal protest over a suicide attack in the northwestern district of Bannu that killed 15 police officers. The attack took place late Saturday in Bannu, near the border with Afghanistan. Pakistani authorities blamed the attack on T Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump administration says its war in Iran has been ‘terminated’ before 60-day deadline
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Prosecution of ex-FBI chief Comey over seashell post is flawed, experts say
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Paramedics help passenger give birth just before Delta flight lands
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump safe after shooting at White House correspondents dinner, suspect in custody
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Empty tankers heading to US for oil, gas: Trump
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Kamala Harris says she might run for president again in 2028
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: North American farmers pinch pennies on farm machinery
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US experts say American strikes on Iran may amount to war crimes
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Hopes dim for swift end to Iran war after Trump speech
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump interested in calling on Arab states to help pay for Iran war, White House says
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US resumes embassy operations in Venezuela
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Lucrative bets that anticipated Trump's policy surprises warrant scrutiny, experts say
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Death toll rises to 14 in Pakistan suicide attack
item.description: The death toll from a suicide attack on a security post in northwest Pakistan rose to 14 police officers, authorities said early Sunday. A self-proclaimed breakaway group of the Pakistan Taliban has claimed the attack, reports AP. A suicide bomber and several gunmen detonated an explosives-laden Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Indian sailor killed, 4 others injured in ship fire near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: An Indian sailor has been killed and four others injured after a dhow caught fire and sank off the Strait of Hormuz, reports Gulf News, citing the Indian Consulate in Dubai. Eighteen Indian crew members were aboard in the wooden vessel when the fire broke out on Saturday. After the vessel capsize Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Thalapathy Vijay likely to take oath as Tamil Nadu CM on Sunday
item.description: India’s actor-turned-politician Thalapathy Vijay met Tamil Nadu Governor RV Arlekar on Saturday evening and staked a claim to form the government in the state of the country. Citing sources, NDTV said his oath-taking is set to take place on Sunday. This high-stakes meeting comes as Vijay's Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Suvendu sworn in as West Bengal CM
item.description: Suvendu Adhikari has been sworn in as chief minister of West Bengal, marking a decisive political shift in a state long defined by ideological churn, NDTV reports. He took the oath on Saturday, marking a political journey carrying a sense of symmetry and rupture in equal measure, the Indian news o Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: South Korea heads to local elections under shadow of disgraced former president
item.description: South Korea's martial law scandal is set to loom large over local elections in June that will test whether opposition conservatives can rein in the power of the ruling party of President Lee Jae Myung. The June 3 contest for mayors and governors in South Korea's 16 cities and provinces is the firs Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Suvendu Adhikari’s aide shot dead
item.description: Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) leader Suvendu Adhikari’s personal assistant has been shot dead in West Bengal’s Madhyamgram, days after the Indian state’s assembly election results. The victim, identified as Chandranath Rath, was shot twice in the constituency in North 24 Parganas Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: China to probe deadly fireworks factory blast
item.description: Senior Chinese officials took charge of rescue and relief efforts after a fireworks factory explosion killed 26 and injured 61, flattening buildings and burying people in mounds of debris, as they pledged to punish those responsible. Smoke still billowed from the plant two days after Monday's powe Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Modi's party set to make big gains in Indian state elections
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's party was on course to win two of four crucial state elections on Monday, expanding its influence and weakening its main rival half way into his third term in office. Modi's Bharatiya Janata Party was set to return to power in the eastern state of Assam for a Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Modi’s poll gains point to push on India's civil law reform, infrastructure
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s party was set on Monday to record substantial victories in state elections, an outcome that could accelerate its landmark policies like uniform civil laws and ​infrastructure building, political leaders and analysts said. The gains also underline that Mo Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: West Bengal polls: BJP leads in 195 seats, TMC at 92
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) of India has surged ahead in around two-thirds of seats in the West Bengal legislative assembly elections, pointing to a dominant position in the race. According to the Election Commission of India website, the BJP is leading in 195 seats, while Trinamool Congress Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: ‘Numbers fake, wait for sunset’: 5 shocking claims by Mamata
item.description: “Don’t leave the counting centres, the numbers are fake,” Mamata Banerjee said in her first reaction as the Trinamool Congress trailed behind the BJP in the Bengal assembly elections. In a video message released on Monday noon, the TMC supremo urged voters not to leave the counti Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: TVK crosses majority mark in Tamil Nadu, Vijay eyes CM post
item.description: Actor-turned-politician C Joseph Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) has emerged as the single-largest party, with trends showing it leading in 151 seats, Hindustan Times reports. While the All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK)-Congress coalition is ahead in 40 seats and Dravida Mun Delivered by PolitePaul service
